testimonies.title: That's My King — Dr. S.M. Lockridge
testimonies.description: Dr. S.M. Lockridge's legendary sermon declaring the supremacy and sufficiency of Jesus Christ — the King of kings.

testimonies.title: Seeking Allah, Finding Jesus — Nabeel Qureshi
testimonies.description: Nabeel Qureshi shares his journey from devout Islam to faith in Christ through friendship, evidence, and the work of the Holy Spirit.

testimonies.title: Brian 'Head' Welch — I Am Second
testimonies.description: The Korn guitarist shares how Jesus rescued him from a life of addiction and despair, transforming everything.

testimonies.title: Rosaria Butterfield's Testimony
testimonies.description: A former tenured professor of English and women's studies, and a lesbian activist, shares how an unexpected friendship and the Scriptures brought her to repentance and faith.

testimonies.title: Lee Strobel — The Case for Christ
testimonies.description: Former atheist and investigative journalist Lee Strobel set out to disprove Christianity and instead found the evidence overwhelming.

testimonies.title: James Tour — Genius Inventor Discovers His Messiah
testimonies.description: World-renowned nanoscientist Dr. James Tour shares how a Jewish skeptic came to find Jesus as the promised Messiah.

testimonies.title: Lecrae — I Am Second
testimonies.description: Hip-hop artist Lecrae shares his journey from a broken home and reckless living to new life in Christ.

testimonies.title: From Atheist to Christian at Yale — Dr. Paul Lim
testimonies.description: Dr. Paul Lim, a Yale history professor, recounts how God drew a committed atheist to saving faith through intellectual honesty and grace.

testimonies.title: Put God First — Denzel Washington
testimonies.description: Academy Award-winning actor Denzel Washington delivers a powerful commencement address urging graduates to put God first in everything.

testimonies.title: Chris Pratt's 9 Rules — MTV Awards Speech
testimonies.description: Chris Pratt uses his MTV acceptance speech to boldly share his faith: 'God is real. God loves you. God wants the best for you.'

testimonies.title: Bethany Hamilton — I Am Second
testimonies.description: Professional surfer Bethany Hamilton shares how her faith sustained her after losing her arm in a shark attack and how God's purpose never wavered.

testimonies.title: Eric Metaxas — I Am Second
testimonies.description: Bestselling author Eric Metaxas shares his journey from nominal faith to a life-changing encounter with the living God.

testimonies.title: Clayton Kershaw — God, Just Be with Me
testimonies.description: MLB pitcher Clayton Kershaw opens up about finding his identity not in baseball success, but in Christ alone.

testimonies.title: Alex Kendrick — I Am Second
testimonies.description: Filmmaker Alex Kendrick (War Room, Fireproof) shares his testimony and calling to use film for the glory of God.

testimonies.title: Why This Atheist Scientist Became a Believing Christian
testimonies.description: A scientist shares the evidence and encounters that shattered his atheism and led him to faith in Christ.

testimonies.title: Rosaria Butterfield — Set Free
testimonies.description: Rosaria Butterfield shares the story of how radical, ordinary hospitality from a pastor and his wife opened the door to the gospel.

testimonies.title: Anne Wilson — I Am Second
testimonies.description: Christian music artist Anne Wilson shares how the tragic loss of her brother led her to cling to Christ and find her calling in music.

testimonies.title: Ryan Ries — I Am Second
testimonies.description: Ryan Ries shares his testimony of drugs, skateboarding, and the empty pursuit of fame — until Jesus changed everything.

scripture.text: For all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God.
For the wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
But God demonstrates His own love toward us, in that while we were still sinners, Christ died for us.
For God so loved the world, that He gave His only Son, so that everyone who believes in Him will not perish, but have eternal life.
For by grace you have been saved through faith; and this is not of yourselves, it is the gift of God; not as a result of works, so that no one may boast.
Therefore repent and return, so that your sins may be wiped away, in order that times of refreshing may come from the presence of the Lord.
Therefore if anyone is in Christ, this person is a new creation; the old things passed away; behold, new things have come.
If you confess with your mouth Jesus as Lord, and believe in your heart that God raised Him from the dead, you will be saved.
But He was pierced for our offenses, He was crushed for our wrongdoings; the punishment for our well-being was laid upon Him, and by His wounds we are healed.
Jesus said to him, "I am the way, and the truth, and the life; no one comes to the Father except through Me."
Therefore there is now no condemnation at all for those who are in Christ Jesus.
No, I tell you, but unless you repent, you will all likewise perish.
If we confess our sins, He is faithful and righteous, so that He will forgive us our sins and cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
He saved us, not on the basis of deeds which we did in righteousness, but in accordance with His mercy, by the washing of regeneration and renewing by the Holy Spirit.
We must work the works of Him who sent Me as long as it is day; night is coming when no one can work.
The axe is already laid at the root of the trees; therefore every tree that does not bear good fruit is being cut down and thrown into the fire.
For whoever wishes to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for My sake and the gospel's will save it.
So then, none of you can be My disciple who does not give up all his own possessions.
Not everyone who says to Me, 'Lord, Lord,' will enter the kingdom of heaven, but the one who does the will of My Father who is in heaven will enter.
Even so faith, if it has no works, is dead, being by itself.
If you love Me, you will keep My commandments.

After these things I looked, and behold, a great multitude which no one could count, from every nation and all the tribes, peoples, and languages, standing before the throne and before the Lamb, clothed in white robes, and palm branches were in their hands; and they cried out with a loud voice, saying, 'Salvation belongs to our God who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb.'

sections.title: God Is Holy
sections.content: The God who made the universe is perfectly holy, righteous, and just. He created us to know Him and to glorify Him forever. "Holy, Holy, Holy, is the Lord of armies; the whole earth is full of His glory." (Isaiah 6:3, NASB)

sections.title: We Are Sinful
sections.content: Every human being has rebelled against God. We have broken His commands in thought, word, and deed. Our sin separates us from a holy God and earns us His righteous judgment. "For all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God." (Romans 3:23, NASB)

sections.title: Christ Is the Savior
sections.content: God, in His great love, sent His only Son Jesus Christ to live the perfect life we could not live and to die the death we deserved. On the cross, Jesus bore the full wrath of God for sinners. Three days later, He rose from the dead, conquering sin and death forever. "But God demonstrates His own love toward us, in that while we were still sinners, Christ died for us." (Romans 5:8, NASB)

sections.title: Repent and Believe
sections.content: God commands everyone everywhere to repent — to turn from sin and turn to Him. Salvation is received by grace alone, through faith alone, in Christ alone. It is not earned by good works but freely given to all who trust in Jesus. "Repent and believe in the gospel." (Mark 1:15, NASB)
